name: Michelle Jeon

dental_history.notes: Post-orthodontic patient. All 4 wisdom teeth extracted 2021. Wears Essix retainer nightly. Latex and NSAID allergies — use nitrile gloves, acetaminophen only.

clinical_notes:
CLINICAL NOTES — Michelle Jeon (DOB: 2000-12-28)
Date: 2026-03-15  Provider: Dr. Williams
Chart #: MJ-2026-001

ALLERGIES: LATEX (contact dermatitis), NSAIDs (GI upset).
Use nitrile gloves. Analgesic: acetaminophen only.

PATIENT HISTORY:
- Post-orthodontic (braces 2020-2022). Essix retainer
  worn nightly, good compliance.
- All 4 wisdom teeth surgically extracted Aug 2021,
  healed without complications.
- Sealants placed on #16 and #26 (June 2025).
- Last cleaning Jan 2026 — no issues noted at that time.
- Non-smoker, no medications, vegetarian diet.

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Sharp pain upper right when eating cold
foods (started ~1 week ago). Also concerned about a
"dark spot" she noticed on lower left molar.

CLINICAL FINDINGS:
- Tooth #15: Crack line visible on mesial marginal ridge
  under transillumination. Cold test: sharp, lingering
  pain (8 sec). No periapical radiolucency. Cracked
  tooth syndrome. Recommend ceramic onlay (D2664) to
  prevent fracture propagation.
- Tooth #37: Occlusal pit caries (the "dark spot").
  Early enamel-dentin involvement. Small but definite
  on bitewing. Recommend composite restoration (D2391).
- Tooth #12: Mild facial erosion on incisal edge,
  consistent with acid erosion pattern. No sensitivity.
  Monitor and counsel on dietary acids.
- Tooth #43: Lingual calculus buildup with localized
  gingivitis. Bleeding on probing. Recommend targeted
  scaling and oral hygiene reinforcement.

PERIODONTAL ASSESSMENT:
- Probing depths 1-3mm throughout — generally excellent.
- Localized bleeding #43 lingual, #33 lingual.
- Minimal plaque. Excellent home care overall.
- Orthodontic alignment stable, no relapse noted.

ORTHODONTIC FOLLOW-UP:
- Retainer fit checked: adequate, no adjustments needed.
- Alignment stable since debonding (2022).
- Mild black triangle #31-41 (common post-ortho),
  patient aware, no treatment requested.

TREATMENT PLAN:
1. [IMMEDIATE] Tooth #15 — Ceramic onlay (D2664) to
   stabilize crack before it propagates to pulp
2. [SOON] Tooth #37 — Composite restoration (D2391)
3. [ROUTINE] Tooth #43 — Localized scaling + OHI
4. [MONITOR] Tooth #12 — Erosion monitoring, dietary
   counseling re: citrus and carbonated beverages

RADIOGRAPHIC NOTES:
Bitewing radiographs (4) taken. #15 no periapical
pathology, crack not visible radiographically (clinical
diagnosis). #37 occlusal radiolucency at DEJ. Extraction
sites #18, #28, #38, #48 fully healed. Sealants #16,
#26 intact. No other pathology. Bone levels excellent.

NOTES: Discussed crack on #15 — patient understands risk
of delayed treatment (potential root canal if crack
reaches pulp). Scheduled onlay prep for next week.
#37 restoration to be done same visit if time allows.
Reminder: LATEX ALLERGY — ensure nitrile setup.
NO NSAIDs — post-op pain management with acetaminophen
500mg q6h PRN. Next visit: 1 week.
